source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The single-phase, 50 Hz iron core transformer in the circuit has both the vertical arms of cross-sectional area \(20 \, \text{cm}^2\) and both the horizontal arms of cross-sectional area \(10 \, \text{cm}^2\). If the two windings shown were wound instead on opposite horizontal arms, the mutual inductance will:

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the two windings shown' and asks about moving them to 'opposite horizontal arms', which depends on the diagram layout
The task involves spatial placement of windings on transformer core arms, a visual/structural relation not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record shows an existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: The question combines transformer-core geometry information with a hypothetical change in winding placement
It requires comparing magnetic coupling before and after moving windings, not just reading a single visual fact
Cross-sectional areas of vertical and horizontal arms are both given, suggesting reasoning over at least two structural conditions
There are 4 answer choices, indicating a determinate comparative reasoning task rather than pure extraction
verifiability: The provided answer 'be halved' exactly matches one of the listed choices
This is effectively a multiple-choice question with a discrete expected outcome
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true, so the sample assets and answer are available for downstream checking

decision_reasons: Question, image, and answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
